questions.q: What does the name Pandu literally mean?
questions.explanation: Pandu means pale or white, describing the unusual pallor he bore from birth.

questions.q: Who was Pandu's elder brother?
questions.explanation: Dhritarashtra was the elder brother, passed over for the throne because of his blindness.

questions.q: Who were Pandu's two wives?
questions.explanation: Pandu first married Kunti and later took Madri, the princess of Madra, as his second wife.

questions.q: The five brothers who carry Pandu's name are called what?
questions.explanation: Yudhishthira, Bhima, Arjuna, Nakula, and Sahadeva are the Pandavas, sons of Pandu.

questions.q: Which sage did Pandu fatally shoot while the sage was in the form of a deer?
questions.explanation: Pandu shot the sage Kindama, who was sporting with his mate in the form of a deer.

questions.q: What curse did the dying Kindama lay upon Pandu?
options: He would die the moment he embraced a wife in desire
His sons would never rule a kingdom
He would lose his kingdom in a game of dice
He would be born blind in his next life
questions.explanation: Kindama cursed Pandu to die in the act of love, the very state in which the sage was struck.

questions.q: Who was Pandu's biological father?
questions.explanation: Vyasa fathered Pandu by Ambalika through niyoga to save the dying Kuru line.

questions.q: Why was Pandu crowned king instead of his elder brother?
options: Dhritarashtra's blindness barred him from kingship
A vote of the council chose Pandu
questions.explanation: Dhritarashtra's blindness was held to disqualify him, so the younger Pandu received the crown.

questions.q: Where did Pandu retire to live as an ascetic after the curse?
questions.explanation: He settled with his wives among the hermits of Shatashringa, the hundred-peaked mountain.

questions.q: Whom was Pandu embracing when Kindama's curse finally claimed his life?
questions.explanation: Overcome by desire for Madri, Pandu embraced her and died instantly, as the curse had foretold.

questions.q: Why was Pandu born pale?
options: A curse drained the color from his skin
His mother Ambalika blanched with fear on seeing Vyasa
He was born during a solar eclipse
questions.explanation: Ambalika turned pale at Vyasa's fierce appearance during conception, so her son was born with that pallor.

questions.q: What was the sage Kindama doing when Pandu shot him?
options: Mating with his doe in the form of a deer
questions.explanation: Kindama had taken deer form to mate with his wife, and Pandu's arrows struck them in that act.

questions.q: How many of Pandu's five sons were actually born of his own body?
questions.explanation: Because of the curse, all five Pandavas were fathered by gods invoked through Kunti's mantra.

questions.q: Which god did Pandu ask Kunti to summon first, giving him his eldest son Yudhishthira?
questions.explanation: Pandu chose Dharma first so that his heir would be a son of righteousness itself.

questions.q: Before the curse, what was Pandu famed for as king of Hastinapura?
options: Conquering many kingdoms and filling the treasury
Composing a celebrated code of laws
Digging great canals from the Ganga
questions.explanation: Pandu's victorious campaigns subdued many kings and restored Hastinapura's wealth and prestige.

questions.q: What did Pandu do with his royal life after Kindama's curse?
options: He ruled the kingdom from the forest
He searched for healers to lift the curse
He renounced the throne and lived as an ascetic
He sought a counter-boon from Shiva
questions.explanation: Stricken with remorse, Pandu gave up the kingdom and adopted the austere life of a forest hermit.

questions.q: Why did Pandu beg Kunti to bear sons by invoking the gods?
options: He had learned that the sonless cannot attain the blessed worlds
Bhishma had demanded heirs for the throne
Gandhari's growing brood threatened his line
A dream from his ancestors commanded it
questions.explanation: Pandu pleaded that a man without sons is barred from the higher worlds, so heirs had to be begotten for him.

questions.q: How many arrows did Pandu shoot at the mating deer?
questions.explanation: The epic says Pandu struck the coupling deer with five swift arrows before learning they were a sage and his wife.

questions.q: The influence of which season finally overcame Pandu's restraint on the day he died?
questions.explanation: Wandering with Madri amid the blossoms of spring, Pandu was overpowered by desire despite years of restraint.

questions.q: Who was Pandu's legal father, in whose name he was begotten through niyoga?
questions.explanation: Pandu was raised as the son of Vichitravirya, who had died childless, though Vyasa was his begetter.

questions.q: When Pandu asked Kunti for a fourth son, how did she respond?
options: She demanded that Madri ask first
She summoned the god Dharma once more
She agreed, but the mantra failed her
She refused, saying scripture brands a woman's fourth resort as wanton
questions.explanation: Kunti declined, citing the law that a woman who resorts to a fourth man falls from virtue.

questions.q: After Pandu's death, who escorted his sons and the funeral remains to Hastinapura?
options: The ascetics of the mountain hermitage
questions.explanation: The hermits of Shatashringa brought Kunti, the five boys, and the remains of Pandu and Madri to the Kuru capital.

questions.q: Which king of Magadha did Pandu defeat during his great campaign of conquest?
questions.explanation: The Adi Parva records that Pandu slew Dirgha, the offending king of Magadha, during his digvijaya.

questions.q: Why did the dying Kindama say Pandu's deed deserved a curse?
options: Because deer were sacred to his hermitage
Because Pandu had hunted on holy ground
Because he struck a creature in the act of mating, which even hunters spare
Because Pandu refused to ask the sage's forgiveness
questions.explanation: Kindama did not condemn hunting itself but the cruelty of killing a creature at the moment of union.

questions.q: What did Pandu first tell Kunti and Madri after the curse fell on him?
options: That they should return to Hastinapura while he became a forest ascetic
That they must hide the curse from Bhishma
That they should seek sons by niyoga at once
That the family would soon return to rule the city
questions.explanation: Pandu urged his wives to go back to the capital, but both refused and followed him into the ascetic life.

questions.q: How did the fatal moment between Pandu and Madri come about?
options: Madri begged him for a single embrace
He believed the curse had lapsed after many years
A festival's wine clouded his judgment
Wandering with her amid spring blossoms, he yielded to desire despite her protests
questions.explanation: Alone with Madri in the flowering spring forest, Pandu was seized by desire and embraced her even as she resisted.

questions.q: In which parva of the Mahabharata is Pandu's life and death narrated?
questions.explanation: Pandu's reign, curse, and death all appear in the Adi Parva, the epic's book of beginnings.

questions.q: What special effort did Pandu undertake before the conception of Arjuna?
options: He fasted for a month at the Ganga's source
He won a boon from Shiva in single combat
He stood in year-long penance to win Indra's favor
questions.explanation: Pandu and Kunti observed severe year-long vows to please Indra, who then fathered the peerless Arjuna.

questions.q: At whose urging did Kunti share her god-summoning mantra with Madri?
questions.explanation: Pandu, grieved that Madri remained childless, persuaded Kunti to grant her one use of the mantra.

questions.q: Pandu's story is most often read as a parable about what theme?
options: The destructive power of unmastered desire
questions.explanation: From the hunt that earned the curse to the embrace that fulfilled it, Pandu's fate dramatizes desire overriding restraint.
